title: marketing — retention windows
description: Marketing events are financial records (7 years default, 10 for Enterprise). Deliveries retain 2 years (support-debugging window). Dead letters retain INDEFINITELY (financial audit — every unresolved P1 preserved). Provider configs retain while active + 90d grace after deactivation.
compliance_regimes_referenced: gdpr Art. 17 — right to erasure (with financial-retention carve-out)
gdpr Art. 17 §3 — carve-out: legal obligation to retain financial records supersedes erasure right
pci-dss 10.7 — audit trail retention 1 year hot + 6 years archive
national tax law — 7-year financial-record retention (US, EU, UK, CA baselines)
ad-spend audit — some finance regimes require 10 years

hot.description: Active + first year for ROAS reporting, support queries, dispute resolution

cold.description: Financial-audit retention. Enterprise tenants with marketing_extended_retention entitlement: 3650 (10 years).

purge.trigger: > 7 years past created_at (10 years for Enterprise) AND status IS terminal (delivered / partially_delivered / failed / suppressed_by_consent / superseded)
purge.notes: Events in status='pending' or 'dispatching' are NEVER purged by the retention job — they're the ReconcileMarketingEventStatusJob's problem. A stuck event past the retention window would indicate a decade-old crashed dispatch job.

cold.description: Support-debugging retention. Enterprise does NOT extend delivery retention — the audit trail lives on marketing_events.

purge.notes: Delivery rows are per-attempt log entries. Their support-debugging value drops to zero after 2 years — the retention window is set to the tenant support agreement's SLA + a buffer.

cold.description: Grace period after soft-delete for audit / rollback.

purge.notes: The 90-day grace allows the ops team to review the config change after a compromise or rollback. After grace, the row is hard-purged but the historical config data survives in the audit trail (which retains 7 years) via before/after snapshots on MarketingConfigurationChangedNotification + MarketingProviderConfigured events.

cold.description: Dead-letter rows are NEVER auto-purged. Every unresolved P1 preserved for the tenant's lifetime + beyond.

purge.trigger: TenantErased cascade (via FK CASCADE) — never scheduled/timed
purge.notes: Dead letters are the highest-integrity retention class in the module. A resolved dead letter still lives on — resolution is recorded via resolved_at + resolution_note but the row does not become purgeable. Rationale: every dead letter represents a paid conversion signal that failed to reach an ad network; that's an ad-spend audit trail that regulators, finance teams, and marketing auditors all care about years later.

purge.action: cascaded (blobs are sub-fields, not separate tables)
purge.notes: The snapshots outlive individual consent state changes on the source rows — that's the whole point of the snapshot pattern. The event carries its historical consent + attribution for its lifetime.

request_payload is encrypted at rest (contains PII + potentially sensitive attribution). Encryption keys are managed by the compliance/encryption service; key rotation does not invalidate historical rows (each row carries its key version).

special_cases.action: hard delete of every marketing_events / marketing_deliveries / marketing_provider_configs / marketing_dead_letters row via FK CASCADE
special_cases.notes: Audit trail rows written during those events survive per audit module's outlives-source design. Regulators can still trace the erased tenant's activity — but the tenant's own data is gone.

special_cases.action: RedactUserFromMarketingEventsJob nulls subject_id on affected marketing_events + fires provider-side deletion requests (Meta Data Deletion API, Google Ads user-data deletion, etc.)
special_cases.notes: The event row itself survives (financial retention) but the subject-identifying fields are redacted. Provider-side deletion is best-effort — Stackra records the deletion request; the provider is responsible for honoring it.

special_cases.action: Config is soft-deleted; existing deliveries preserve the provider_config_id reference (RESTRICT FK); the config row is hard-purged after the 90-day grace
special_cases.notes: This is the 'referenced deleted config' path. Deliveries created after config deactivation are impossible (observer refuses). Deliveries created before continue to reference the historical config row via RESTRICT.

cross_reference: The audit rows written by Auditable outlive the source rows per audit::retention.json — audit retention is 7 years regardless of source-row retention.
TenantErased cascades hard-delete via FK CASCADE, but the audit rows written during those events survive the cascade (per audit module's outlives-source design).
The compliance/data-subject-rights module orchestrates UserErased across all modules including this one — the module registers `RedactUserFromMarketingEventsJob` as a compliance callback.
